snippet.title: McDonald's CEO Chris Kempczinski | In Good Company | Norges Bank Investment Management
snippet.description:
In this episode, CEO of NBIM, Nicolai Tangen talks to the CEO of McDonald's, Chris Kempczinski. What’s the secret to McDonald's success globally? How do they use AI in their business? And why are McDonald's fries so good? Tune in and find out! 

At the end of 2022 the fund's holdings in McDonald's had a market value of 2.3 billion dollars. 

The podcast In Good Company is part of Norges Bank Investment Management , a podcast about our investments. We are transparent about how the fund is invested. In this podcast, you to get a deeper insight into the companies the fund is invested in. Our CEO, Nicolai Tangen, has in-depth interviews with the leaders of some of the largest companies in the world. You will get insight into their leadership principles, the company’ strategy and how they are dealing with a large investor like us. You will also learn more about our role as an owner of the companies. 

You can read more about the fund's investments in McDonald's, and more about the episode, here: https://www.nbim.no/en/publications/podcast/chris-kempczinski-ceo-of-mcdonalds/ #InGoodCompany #mcdonalds #NorgesBankInvestmentManagement #ChrisKempczinski #NicolaiTangen  #Oljefondet

snippet.title: CEO Chris K's Morning Routine: A Look Inside the Life of McDonald's Top Executive
snippet.description:
McDonald's CEO Chris Kempczinski shares his morning routine, from waking up at 5:30 AM to working out and eating breakfast. He also talks about how he stays focused and motivated throughout the day.

Kempczinski says that he wakes up at 5:30 AM every day and immediately starts working out. He believes that exercise is essential for staying healthy and focused. After working out, he eats breakfast and then gets ready for work.

Kempczinski says that he tries to stay focused on the big picture and not get bogged down in the details. He also says that he is always looking for ways to improve McDonald's and make it even better.

Kempczinski's morning routine is a glimpse into the life of McDonald's top executive. It shows that he is a dedicated and hardworking leader who is committed to making McDonald's the best it can be.

snippet.title: CEO Chris K Talks to Chef Chad About Keeping McDonald's Food Top Notch
snippet.description:
McDonald's CEO Chris Kempczinski sat down with Chef Chad Schafer, Senior Director of Culinary Innovation, to discuss the company's commitment to providing high-quality food. Chef Chad shared his insights on how McDonald's is constantly innovating to improve its recipes, and he also gave a behind-the-scenes look at how the Quarter Pounder with Cheese is made.

In the interview, Chef Chad talked about the importance of using fresh, high-quality ingredients, and he also discussed the company's efforts to reduce sodium and calories in its menu items. He also shared some of his favorite McDonald's recipes, including the new Big Mac Sauce dip cup.

CEO Kempczinski was impressed with Chef Chad's passion for food, and he thanked him for his dedication to keeping McDonald's food top notch. He also said that he was excited about the company's future plans to continue innovating and providing customers with delicious and nutritious food.

snippet.title: Cuban Immigrants Franchise Their Own McDonald's
snippet.description:
McDonald's CEO, Chris Kempczinski, interviews Carlos Mateos, Jr., whose parents immigrated to the U.S. from Cuba and worked tirelessly to franchise their own McDonald’s.

Today, Carlos is following in their footsteps, bringing passion and leadership to his restaurants in the Washington, D.C. area.

snippet.title: McDonald's will begin layoffs in April, says CEO
snippet.description:
McDonald's will begin layoffs in April, says CEO

McDonald’s Corp. CEO Chris Kempczinski confirmed Wednesday that the fast-food chain will begin layoffs in April, but he did not provide details on how many people will be affected.

Kempczinski said during a call with analysts that the company is “making some necessary reductions” in staffing and in other areas as it works to adjust to the economic fallout from the coronavirus pandemic. He said the cuts will come in April and will affect restaurants and the corporate office.

McDonald’s, which has more than 14,000 restaurants in the U.S., has been hit hard by the pandemic, with sales declining sharply in recent months. The company has temporarily closed some locations and reduced its hours of operation at others.

The company said earlier this month that it had applied for federal aid through the Paycheck Protection Program, part of the government’s $2 trillion stimulus package. The program provides loans to small businesses that maintain their payrolls during the pandemic.

McDonald’s said it would use the funds to help keep employees on the payroll and cover other costs, including rent and utilities. The company said it would also be offering “additional financial

snippet.title: McDonalds Spends Big In Recessive Environment and Profits Soar
snippet.description:
Source:
https://www.podbean.com/eau/pb-srxqu-144ec35

'The Recession is coming, the recession is coming!!' the outcry echoes across the news. 
Yet, McDonalds' sales increased by 12.6%, indicating strong demand. 
In the latest earnings call, CEO Chris Kempczinski highlighted the importance of 'Accelerating the organisation.' And outlined management's plan to increase spending on digitalisation to help implement new digital tools and platforms. The benefit, streamlined tasks for the employees freeing up time for manages to analyse the changing environment and adjust and adapt. 
If you are not growing, you are dying. 
Moreover, while others cower, you create relationships with a wider market with less competition and more importantly they form the habit of working with you. 
While fortune favours the brave, courage is really about operating with commonsense. 
There is no fear but fear itself. While others show fear when the market is offering a sale, you step forward and own the market. 
 
The benefits of engaging DamianAndrews.com include:
Increased Revenue and Profitability
Greater Competitive Advantage
Improved Operational Efficiency
Enhanced Customer Satisfaction
Deeper Employee Engagement and Productivity
Higher Talent Attraction and Retention
More Adaptability and Innovation
Better Long-Term Business Success. 

http://www.DamianAndrews.com

snippet.title: McDonald's Sustainability Efforts: A One-on-One with Director of Global Supply Chain
snippet.description:
Join McDonald's CEO Chris Kempczinski in a one-on-one interview with Cesar Pina, Senior Director of Global Supply Chain for 80 international markets. Learn how McDonald's is making strides towards sustainability in the fast food industry.

#esg, #sustainability, #supplychain, #ceointerview, #globalsupplychain

snippet.title: McDonald's Supply Chain is Inclusive of All Businesses
snippet.description:
McDonald's supply chain is inclusive of woman, minority, veteran, LGBTQ and disabled business owners. McDonald's Senior Director of Global Supply Chain Business Diversity, Karmetria Burton, explains more about her efforts to McDonald's CEO, Chris Kempczinski. 

#womanowned #womeninbusiness #minorityowned #LGBTQ #inclusive #supplychain #diversity

snippet.title: Disney CEO Bob Iger sits down with Bob Myers | Lead by Example
snippet.description:
Despite all of his success as CEO of Disney, Bob Iger has an underdog mentality. Bob Myers sits down with him to examine his leadership journey and how the development of his strong work ethic early in life led to his success. They discuss his negotiations to purchase Pixar, Marvel and Lucasfilm, as well as Disney’s shift to streaming and the risk that went along with that decision. Iger also reveals his desire to own an NBA team one day, and gives a hint at the type of owner he would be. This episode was recorded prior to Bob Iger returning as CEO of Disney.

0:00 Bob Myers sits down with Bob Iger
1:21 Building a strong work ethic early in life
9:02 Not leading from a “fear position”
13:42 Confidence coming from underdog mentality
18:22 Pressure of Pixar acquisition
23:34 Convincing Steve Jobs, Ike Permutter and George Lucas to sell
38:42 Disney’s shift to streaming
45:04 Goal to own an NBA team
#ESPN #LeadByExample #Podcast #BobIger #Disney #espn  

✔️Subscribe to ESPN+ http://espnplus.com/youtube
✔️ Get the ESPN App: http://www.espn.com/espn/apps/espn
✔️Subscribe to ESPN on YouTube: http://es.pn/SUBSCRIBEtoYOUTUBE

snippet.title: Bjørn Gulden CEO of Adidas | Podcast | In Good Company | Norges Bank Investment Management
snippet.description:
Bjørn Gulden CEO of Adidas: Shoes, Leadership, and Fast Decisions.
The Norwegian former football player Bjørn Gulden share unique insight into how it was to leave Puma for the main competitor Adidas. He also explains his plan to rebuild Adidas, the relationship with Kanye West and why he makes quick decisions. 

In this episode, Bjorn Gulden, share his insights and experiences with Nicolai Tangen, CEO of Norges Bank Investment Management. 

The podcast In Good Company is part of Norges Bank Investment Management , a podcast about our investments. We are transparent about how the fund is invested. In this podcast, you to get a deeper insight into the companies the fund is invested in. 

Our CEO, Nicolai Tangen, has in-depth interviews with the leaders of some of the largest companies in the world. You will get insight into their leadership principles, the company’s strategy and how they are dealing with a large investor like us. You will also learn more about our role as an owner of the companies. 

You can read more about the podcast on https://www.nbim.no/en/publications/podcast/ #adidas 

#BjørnGulden #football #Oljefondet #InGoodCompany #NorgesBankInvestmentManagement

snippet.title: 143 David Macdonald, Ex-CEO Discovery Japan
snippet.description:
David Macdonald was originally a JET in Japan in 1995.  He joined NTT Docomo in 1999 and then joined Walt Disney Internet Group Asia Pacific in 2004.  He moved to YouTube Japan in 2009 becoming Head of YouTube Spaces Asia Pacific in 2014.  He became President of Discovery Japan in 2018 and President and Representative Officer, Discovery Japan GK in 2020.

David mentioned the speaker who got him on the “complain, confront or conform” terminology can be found at www.juliensbourrelle.com.

The definition of leadership he likes is: “Leadership is the art of getting someone else to do something you want done because he/she wants to do it”.

snippet.title: Media stocks move higher following departure of CNN CEO Chris Licht
snippet.description:
Brian Stelter, media commentator and former CNN anchor, and CNBC.com media reporter Alex Sherman, join 'The Exchange' to discuss the departure of CNN's Chairman and CEO Chris Licht, headwinds facing CNN, and the potential for a sale of CNN.
